title: PactPilot Amendment
description: An append-only scope change to a base pact. One file per amendment; files are create-only. When amendments conflict on a path, the latest by commit ancestry wins — never ids or timestamps.

scopeGlob.description: A PactPilot glob v1 scope expression (SPEC §5): repo-root-relative, anchored, `/`-separated segments of literals and `*`, with `**` only as a whole segment. Reserved characters (? [ ] { } ! \ and controls), `.`/`..` segments, empty segments, and leading/trailing slashes are invalid. This pattern enforces the character-level rules; SPEC §5.1 is normative.

schemaVersion.description: Major-only schema version. Files with an unknown major are invalid.

id.description: Human-chosen slug plus a 6-hex-char entropy suffix. Must equal the filename (without .json). Carries no ordering semantics.

pactId.description: Id of the base pact this amends. Must equal the parent directory name.

description.description: Why the pact was changed. Required — the audit trail is the point. Surfaced in the PR check summary.

addedScope.description: Scope globs (SPEC §5) added to the pact's scope. Temporal rules (commit ancestry) decide whether covered work earns Green or Yellow. Within this amendment, addedScope beats removedScope.

removedScope.description: Scope globs (SPEC §5) removed from the pact's scope. A removal stands: a PR diff touching an effectively removed path is Red. Conflicts across amendments resolve latest-by-ancestry. An invalid glob here makes the whole file invalid (Red) — skipping it would fail open.
